prior_handoff.required_next_step: wire corpus discovery into a commit-bound effective-queue gate with independent discovered-file-count agreement

memory_recovered.statement: The effective operational queue is a derived view, not a single file: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json supplies the canonical baseline while validated operations/queue-updates records supply later append-only evolution.
memory_recovered.selection_rule: A team must not select work from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone when validated later owned records exist.
memory_recovered.authority_rule: Neither source silently overwrites the other; contradictions are surfaced and classified before precedence is applied.

inputs: canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json bytes and blob SHA
complete discovered set of operations/queue-updates/*.json paths
parsed update records with blob SHAs

required_checks.check: duplicate_item_ids_are_retained_as_versions_and resolved only by explicit timestamp and provenance rules
unresolved_contradictions_are emitted and cannot be converted to completed or active state silently

gate_contract.acceptance_condition: gate_status is accepted only when all checks pass, discovered and parsed counts agree, no rejected records exist, and no unresolved precedence contradiction affects task selection

observed: The canonical queue blob still ends continuity history at M-001.
The append-only M-008 record exists and explicitly requires a commit-bound effective-queue gate.
Recent commits continue to add queue and research state after the canonical queue blob was last updated.
inferred: Without count agreement and commit binding, a derived queue can appear internally valid while omitting records or mixing repository moments.
proposed: Implement this contract as a deterministic Node gate and invoke it in CI before any autonomous team selects its next queue item.
superseded: Treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as the complete current queue.
Accepting a caller-supplied subset of queue-update records without repository discovery and count reconciliation.
unresolved: The executable gate and CI workflow integration are not yet committed.
A complete live repository directory enumeration was not exposed by the connector in this run, so no corpus count is claimed.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_conflict: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json presents M-001 as latest while append-only records present later continuity items.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: temporal layering: canonical baseline plus later evolution log
contradictions_resolved.remaining_risk: selection remains unsafe until a complete commit-bound reconciliation gate is executable

falsification_route: Remove one discovered queue-update record and require count mismatch rejection.
Mix canonical and update blobs from different commits and require source binding rejection.
Provide two same-item versions with ambiguous precedence and require rejection with an emitted contradiction.
Provide a malformed or unsupported record and require fail-closed rejection rather than omission.
next_action: Implement a deterministic effective-queue gate from this contract, add adversarial tests, and wire it into CI before autonomous queue selection.
